name: Red Sea / Suez Canal
impact: Over 70% of shipping traffic has rerouted via the Cape of Good Hope, adding 10–14 days to transit times and increasing fuel and insurance costs by an estimated $1M+ per voyage.

name: Black Sea
impact: Black Sea grain corridor suspended. Tanker insurance premiums increased 300%. Ukrainian grain exports reduced by 40%, threatening food security in 40+ import-dependent countries.

name: Strait of Hormuz
impact: Heightened Iranian military posturing has raised risk premiums. No closures to date but insurance rates elevated. A closure scenario would trigger immediate global oil price spike of 30–50%.

name: Gulf of Aden
dailyTransit: Connects Red Sea to Indian Ocean
impact: Houthi drone and missile attacks have targeted over 84 commercial vessels. Major shipping lines including Maersk, MSC, and CMA CGM suspended Red Sea operations, causing severe supply chain delays.

name: Baltic Sea Pipelines
impact: Nord Stream 1 and 2 pipelines were sabotaged in September 2022. 55 BCM/year of natural gas capacity to Europe permanently destroyed, forcing Europe to find alternative LNG sources at 2-3x cost.
